forecaster_note: Surfline model read (Sun 19 Jul): the unseasonal ENE swell forecast earlier in the week has filled in — ~7.6ft @ 11s from 77°, right in the 30–80° sweet spot — with a long-period S groundswell (2.5ft @ 17s) running underneath. Energy is high (~950 kJ) but a light SE side-onshore stops it being clean today, so the early window is your friend. Monday tidies right up under a light W offshore as the swell eases back to a fair-good head-high. Water 17°C. (Note: shark detection feed was offline this refresh — check the live SharkSmart map before paddling out.)

wind.summary: Light SE 15 kph (gusts 22) — side/cross-onshore but gentle. Cleanest early; the exposed beaches will bump up as it builds through the day, sheltered corners hold better.

shark.summary: SharkSmart detection feed was unavailable this refresh (server returned 503) — no all-clear can be confirmed. Tap the live map to check current detections before you paddle out.

days.label: Today
days.note: The ENE swell has landed — solid, unseasonal energy (~950 kJ) at 7.6ft/11s from 77°, dead in your 30–80° window, with a long-period S line underneath. Size is up across the board but a light SE side-onshore is the catch: Dee Why Point and Manly's corner hold shape best (fair), Long Reef and Curl Curl get bumpy despite the size. Surf early before the wind builds.

dee-why.window: The 77° ENE swell is dead-on for the Point's rights and there's real energy behind it. Light SE is side-onshore — the headland gives the Point partial shelter, so it holds shape better than the open beaches. Get in early; high tide 11:59am keeps the bank full through late morning before the wind firms up.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: This ENE angle is exactly what the Point wants — sit wide on the rights and use the 11s period to pick the sets that stand up cleanest before the SE puts texture on it.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — the reef grabs the full ENE energy at 4–5ft. Fully exposed to the SE though, so expect bump on the faces. Worth it if you want size and paddle power over polish; early is cleaner.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Plenty of push here — step up a board and commit early on the sets before the side-shore chop makes the drop fussy.

curl-curl.window: Solid size (3–5ft) but the most exposed of the group to the SE — power without much shape most of the day. Best in the first hour before the wind gets into it.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Raw and open to the wind — pick the shifting peaks and don't get greedy; it's a wave-count session unless you catch it early and glassy.

manly.window: Best wind shelter of the four — the southern corner takes the sting out of the SE, so North Steyne stays the cleanest option through the day. Smaller than Long Reef but far more rideable if the exposed beaches turn to mush.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Tuck into the corner where the SE is blocked — cleaner walls here than anywhere else today, good for linking turns on your backhand.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: The pick of the run if you can swing a Monday: the ENE swell eases only slightly (still head-high, kJ ~590) and the wind flips to a light W offshore — clean across all four. Fair-good everywhere, with Dee Why Point and Long Reef the standouts.

dee-why.window: Light W offshore grooms the leftover ENE swell — clean head-high walls on the Point's rights. Not as much size as today but far better quality. This is the session to plan for.

long-reef.window: Holds the most size into Monday and the W offshore finally lets it show shape — overhead-plus and clean off the reef. Step-up territory.

curl-curl.window: Offshore clean-up transforms it from today's mess — fun head-high peaks with the ENE swell still feeding in.

manly.window: Clean and playful under the W offshore — smaller than the reefs but tidy walls at North Steyne. Easy, high-count session.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Beyond the reliable 6-day window — this is a trend estimate, not a live forecast (today is already Sunday, so 'Tomorrow' covers this weekend's remaining day). The current E/ENE swell keeps easing through the week; by next weekend expect small, cleaner surf with a W–WSW offshore holding it together. Long Reef best-exposed if any residual energy lingers. Re-check midweek once it's inside the forecast horizon.

alerts: ⚠️ Beyond the 6-day forecast horizon — trend estimate only, not scraped live. Confirm midweek.
dee-why.window: Trend estimate: swell well down from this week's ENE pulse, likely small and clean under a W offshore. Low-key at the Point.

alerts: ⚠️ Beyond the 6-day forecast horizon — trend estimate only, not scraped live. Confirm midweek.
long-reef.window: Trend estimate: best-exposed of the four if any swell lingers into next weekend — reef holds residual energy and the offshore keeps it clean.

alerts: ⚠️ Beyond the 6-day forecast horizon — trend estimate only, not scraped live. Confirm midweek.
curl-curl.window: Trend estimate: small and clean under the offshore — a fun-size beachie session if the residual swell holds.

alerts: ⚠️ Beyond the 6-day forecast horizon — trend estimate only, not scraped live. Confirm midweek.
manly.window: Trend estimate: likely the smallest — the corner blocks most of what little swell is left. Clean but minimal.
